item.title: 什么是着陆页，设计中应包含哪些内容？ | What is a landing page and what should be included in the design?
item.description:
数字营销策略中的 ALanding Page 是为正在进行的活动创建的网站。 通常，它是您现有网站上的子页面。 用户通过单击消息、电子邮件、Google Ads 或社交网络帖子中的链接到达那里。 可以为特定的广告活动或网站的永久元素特意创建登录页面。 在后一种情况下，它的任务是影响整个站点对特定关键字的位置。

无论性质如何，着陆页最重要的任务是转化。 它可能是购买产品或服务，填写联系表格，或者拨打电话或订阅。 您已经对什么是着陆页有所了解。 尽管如此，为了实用和实用，它必须包含以下元素：

登陆页面应该包括什么？

醒目的标题

这个登陆页面元素将首先吸引用户的注意力。 在标题中，告知客户使用您的报价的好处，例如电动滑板车——本周便宜 30%！ 现在给我们打电话！ 也可以是特定的产品特性，例如使用寿命延长 75% 的新一代锂镉电池！

清晰的报价描述

每个网站都应包含专业的书面内容。 描述必须简短，重点是收件人可以快速判断他是否在正确的位置。 因此，您的目标网页应鼓励客户进行转化，例如购买产品、拨打电话或填写表格。 确保您提供的信息提示您采取行动。 确保添加查询信息。

高品质照片

请记住，人们是用眼睛购买的，尤其是在互联网上。 如果目的是销售产品或服务，则网站必须包含高质量的图片或视频。 您还可以为其他类型的转换应用精美的插图或信息图表。

注意正确的安排。 如果您销售儿童玩具，请确保随附其他日常用品。 这将使买家能够快速识别尺寸并协助做出选择。 此外，请注意照片的可扩展性。

如今，以 3D 形式呈现对象越来越流行，这使您可以从各个方面查看产品。 一些摄影师专门从事 3D 产品摄影。 但是，如果没有专业设备，它们更难服用。

号召性用语 (CTA)

这是您需要放在目标网页上的最重要的元素之一。 它的任务是鼓励您执行您想要的特定操作。 如果页面比屏幕尺寸长，它应该出现在几个地方。

联系方式

确保提供联系方式，例如电话号码、电子邮件、地址。 如果目标是销售产品，请提供有关购买过程和交货等待期的信息。 联系方式使报价具有可信度。 在社交媒体上发布指向您的个人资料的链接。 尝试使手机和电子邮件在移动版本中可点击。 该技巧将使客户更容易快速拨打您的电话号码并拨打电话。

强调好处

在采取行动后确定客户的利益是值得的。 您可以列出它们或使用带有照片的滑块显示它们。 信息必须明确无误、真实且令人鼓舞。

构建登陆页面的 7 个步骤

设定明确的广告系列目标

设置明确的广告系列目标将使您能够确定目标网页上应该包含哪些内容。 以下是一些广告系列目标的示例。

  • 增加销售额
  • 公司宣传
  • 获得有关产品的新意见
  • 获得新客户

创建登陆页面项目
在设计着陆页之前，先在互联网上寻找灵感。 在搜索引擎中输入“2022 年前 20 个登陆页面”或“最佳登陆页面”。 您可以在一张普通的纸上开始创建，然后从绘制元素开始。 将完成的布局转移到网站上，看看它看起来如何近似。 如果您满意，请在正确的页面上开始实施该项目。

写下文字

与所有网站一样，登录页面必须包含引人入胜且格式适当的文本。 您可以编写文本并自行组织或外包给专业公司。 以下是要点。

  • 标题 H2 和 H3
  • 有价值的内容——包含关键短语
  • 客户利益
  • 简单明了的信息
  • 号召性用语 - CTA
  • 联系方式

添加专业的图形和照片。

您会在互联网上找到许多免费门户网站，您可以从中轻松下载照片和图形，例如，Pixabay。 如果您的目标网页涉及产品的销售，您显然需要这些照片。 请记住，它们必须具有良好的质量，因为它们是网站最重要的元素之一。 如果您没有图片或照片质量较差，请聘请专业的产品摄影师。 许多照相馆还提供出色的 3D 图像和交互式全景图。 这肯定会增加潜在客户的承诺。

定义号召性用语

号召性用语——这些是执行特定操作的用户调用。 您使用的 CTA 取决于您网站的目的和登录页面的类型。 精心编写的 CTA 是您的目标网页有效的保证。 以下是您可以使用的一些号召性用语示例：

  • 立即购买
  • 学到更多
  • 订阅我们的时事通讯并获取您的代码
  • 立即安装我们的应用程序
  • 立即升级并获得 25% 的折扣
  • 告诉我们您的想法并享受免费试用

添加指向社交媒体和其他网站的链接。

请记住将社交链接添加到您的登录页面。 这很有用，因为访问者还可以开始在选定的社交媒体上关注您的个人资料，无论他们是否购买产品。

发布和测试

在发布之前，值得检查表单、按钮和链接的所有功能。 更正后的页面已准备好发布。 然而，这还不是全部。 立即执行实时测试。 您有一些时间，因为 Google 需要几个小时才能将页面添加到其索引中。 所以，一开始，它不会对观众可见。 您现在可以进行更正。 您可以与朋友分享登录页面并寻求反馈。 测试功能并优化速度以实现最佳用户体验。

概括

如您所见，创建登录页面并不是一项极其困难的任务，并且在大多数情况下规则是相似的。 请记住，登陆页面很重要，因为它是第一个在公众面前代表您的公司或机构的页面。 值得花时间在它上面，让它的所有组件和功能都满足参观者的期望。 如果您居住在爱尔兰，您可以自己创建一个登录页面，或将这项工作委托给都柏林或您所在位置附近的专业网页设计机构。



原文:


ALanding Page in Digital Marketing Strategy is a website created for an ongoing campaign. Usually, it is a subpage on your existing website. The user gets there by clicking on a link in a message, email, Google Ads, or social networks post. A landing page can be deliberately created for a specific advertising campaign or a permanent element of your website. In the latter case, its task is to influence the position of the entire site for particular keywords.

Regardless of the nature, the most important mission of a landing page is conversion. It may be purchasing a product, or service, filling out a contact form, or making a phone call or subscription. You already know a little what a landing page is. Still, to be functional and practical, it must contain the following elements:

What should a landing page consist of?

Eye-catching headline

This landing page element will attract the user’s attention in the first place. In the header, inform about the customer’s benefits using your offer, e.g., Electric scooters – 30% cheaper this week! Call us now! It can also be specific product features, e.g., new generation lithium cadmium batteries with a 75% longer service life!

A clear offer description

Each website should contain professionally written content. The description must be short, to the point so that the recipient can quickly judge if he is in the right place. As a result, your landing page should encourage the customer to convert, e.g., buy a product, make a phone call, or fill out a form. Make sure the information you provide prompts you to take action. Ensure to add the information for inquiry.

High-quality photos

Remember that people buy with their eyes, especially on the internet. If the purpose is to sell a product or service, the website must contain high-quality pictures or videos. You can also apply beautiful illustrations or infographics for other types of conversion.

Take care of the right arrangement. If you sell toys is for children, make sure that it is accompanied by another item of everyday use. This will allow the buyer to quickly recognise the sizes and assist in making a choice. Also, take care of the scalability of photos.

Presenting objects in 3D is more and more popular today, which allows you to see the product from every side. Some photographers specialise in 3D product photography. However, they are more difficult to take without professional equipment.

Call to Action (CTA)

This is one of the most essential elements you need to put on your landing page. Its task is to encourage you to perform the specific action you want. If the page is longer than the screen size, it should appear in several places.

Call to action is a slogan to act, for example, to make a purchase “Buy now”, “subscribe to our newsletter”, or “Fill out the form and get the code now!”, “Update today for 50% Off”.

Contact details

Ensure to provide contact details such as telephone number, email, address. If the aim is selling a product, provide information about the purchasing process and the waiting period for delivery. Contact details give credibility to the offer. Post links to your profiles on social media. Try to make the phone and email clickable in the mobile version. That trick will make it easier for the customer to dial your phone number quickly and make the call.

Emphasise the benefits

It is worthwhile to identify the client’s benefits after taking action. You can list them or present them using a slider with photos. The message must be unmistakable, authentic and encouraging.

7 steps to building a landing page

Set a clear campaign goal

Setting a clear campaign goal will allow you to determine what content should be on the landing page. Here are some examples of campaign goals.

  • increasing sales
  • company promotion
  • getting new opinions about the product
  • gaining new customers

Create a Landing Page project
Look for inspiration on the internet before designing your landing page. Type “top 20 landing pages in 2022” or “best landing pages” into the search engine. You can start creating on a plain sheet of paper and begin by drawing out the elements. Transfer the finished layout to the website and see how it looks approximate. If you are satisfied, start implementing the project on the right page.

Write the text

A landing page must contain engaging and appropriately formatted text like all websites. You can write a text and organise it yourself or outsource it to a professional company. Here are the essentials.

  • H1 heading – eye-catching and containing keywords
  • Headers H2 and H3
  • Valuable content – containing key phrases
  • Customer benefits
  • The simple and clear message
  • Call to Action – CTA
  • Contact details

Add professional graphics and photos.

You will find many free portals on the internet from which you can easily download photos and graphics, e.g. Pixabay. If your landing page concerns the sale of a product, you obviously need to have these photos. Remember that they must be of good quality because they are one of the most essential elements of the website. If you don’t have pictures or the photos are poor quality, hire a professional product photographer. Many photo studios also offer great 3D images and interactive panoramas. Which definitely increases the commitment of the potential client.

Define Call to Action

Call to action – these are user calls to perform a specific action. The CTA you use depends on the purpose of your website and the type of landing page. A well-written CTA is a guarantee that your landing page will be effective. Here are some Call to Action examples you can use:

  • Buy now
  • Learn more
  • Sign for our newsletter and get your code
  • Install our App now
  • Upgrade now and get a 25% discount
  • Tell us what do you think and enjoy a free trial

Add links to social media and other sites.

Remember to add social links to your landing page. This is useful because the visitor can additionally start following your profiles on selected social media, regardless of whether they buy the product or not.

Publish and test

Before publishing, it is worth checking all the functionalities of forms, buttons and links. Your page after corrections is ready for publication. However, that is not all. Perform live tests now. You have some time because Google needs several hours to add a page to its index. So, in the beginning, it will not be visible to the audience. You can make corrections now. You can share the landing page with your friends and ask for feedback. Test functionality and optimise for speed to achieve optimal user experience.

Summary

As you can see, creating a landing page is not an extremely difficult task, and the rules are similar in most cases. Remember that the landing page is significant because it is the first to represent your company or institution in front of the public. It is worth spending time on it to make all its components and functions meet visitors’ expectations. If you live in Ireland, you can create a landing page yourself or delegate this job to a professional web design agency in Dublin or close to your location.
item.author: philipmcnill

item.title: 如何在 JS 中使用默认函数参数值 | How To Use Default Function Parameter Values In JS
item.description:
安德鲁·厄舍

发表于函数参数在 JavaScript 中默认是未定义的。 有时，您想在这种情况下定义一个默认参数。 在ES6 （也称为 ES2015）之前，创建默认参数有点繁琐：

 function createName ( firstName , lastName ) { firstName = typeof firstName === ' undefined ' ? ' Jane ' : firstName ; secondName = typeof secondName === ' undefined ' ? ' Doe ' : secondName ; return firstName + ' ' + secondName ; } console . log ( createName ()); // Jane Doe

通过在 ES6 中引入默认参数值，上述可以简化为：

 function createName ( firstName = ' Jane ' , lastName = ' Doe ' ) { return firstName + ' ' + secondName ; } console . log ( createName ()); // Jane Doe

清洁代码工作室 - Apr 12 4月12日



原文:

Andrew Usher

Posted on May 8

Function parameters are undefined by default in JavaScript. Sometimes, you want to define a default parameter in this case. Before ES6 (also known as ES2015), creating default paramaters was a little tedious:

function createName(firstName, lastName) { firstName = typeof firstName === 'undefined' ? 'Jane' : firstName; secondName = typeof secondName === 'undefined' ? 'Doe' : secondName; return firstName + ' ' + secondName;
} console.log(createName()); // Jane Doe

With the introduction of default parameter values in ES6, the above could be simplified to:

function createName(firstName = 'Jane', lastName = 'Doe') { return firstName + ' ' + secondName;
} console.log(createName()); // Jane Doe

Clean Code Studio - Apr 12
item.author: andrewusher

item.title: Telegram bot + GoogleSheets：解决一些问题 | Telegram bot + GoogleSheets: solving some problems
item.description:
如果您正在开发 Google 表格插件，那么您就是在编写 Google 应用脚本 (GAS) 代码。 因此，这些机器人的大脑可能是用 GAS 编写的。 有一个想法是编写一个机器人，它会在聊天消息中查找关键字并使用这些消息做一些事情。 例如，向我发送通知，告知有人在我感兴趣的主题上写了一些东西。 或者，相反，删除淫秽语言和垃圾邮件，并在聊天中禁止此类消息的作者。

与往常一样，在计划的实际执行过程中，会出现陷阱。 我将在下面讨论其中一些以及绕过它们的方法。 但首先，一些理论。

你用什么语言为你的机器人编写大脑并不重要——GAS、PHP、Pyton 或其他东西。 在任何情况下，您都在使用Telegram API 。
该 API 目前支持两种处理机器人更新的方法： getUpdates和setWebhook 。
例如，您运行getUpdates并获取在那一小时内写在聊天中的所有消息。 在 GAS 中，您可以使用此间隔设置时间触发器。 但这意味着对于我们的反垃圾邮件机器人示例，垃圾邮件将在聊天中挂起一个小时而不受惩罚。 这不好，建议在接近时立即发射垃圾邮件。
因此有必要使用setWebhook 。 正如Marvin's Wonderful Guide to All Things Webhook 中所述：
使您的机器人不必频繁请求更新。
  • 避免在代码中使用任何轮询机制。

    • 现在谈谈接收更新的机制，或者更简单地说，来自 Telegram 聊天的消息。

    1. 我们在 GAS 编辑器中编写最简单的脚本：

    function doPost(e) { var contents = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents); var chat_id = contents.message.from.id; // code for extracting data and writing to the sheet

    就是这样，我们在内容变量中进行了更新。 在进一步处理的帮助下，可以从中提取所需的一切。 例如， chat_id是消息发送者的标识符， text是消息的文本。
    然后您可以将这些数据写在一张纸上，发送给某人等。

    1. 让我们将脚本部署为 Web 应用程序。
      同时，我们指定参数“运行身份：代表我，谁有权访问：所有人”，并将链接复制到 Web 应用程序 (webAppUrl)。

    2. 我们形成一个链接。 为此，您需要连接机器人令牌和 Web 应用程序 URL，如下所示：

      var token = "1234567890:ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"; var webAppUrl = "https://script.google.com/macros/s/XXXXXXXX/exec"; var telegramUrl = "https://api.telegram.org/bot" + token;

      获取 url 并设置 webhook：
      function setWebhook() { var url = telegramUrl + "/setWebhook?url=" + webAppUrl; var response = UrlFetchApp.fetch(url);

      无需任何脚本即可在浏览器中访问此 url 并查看
      {"ok":true,"result":true,"description":"Webhook is already set"}

      这是一个 JSON 对象，解析它，您可以分别提取所有 3 个字段 - ok 、 result和description 。 然后它们可以用于条件逻辑，向用户展示等等。

      陷阱

      所以我们有一个脚本，可以立即将 Telegram 聊天中的所有消息写入工作表。 然后您可以在其中搜索关键字并对这些消息进行处理。 但是想象一下，您高兴地在公共领域发布了指向您的这个机器人的链接，并期望满意的用户会感谢您提供的有用工具。

      它不在那里！

      每个人都对 Google 服务感到满意。 它们为您提供工作表、脚本开发环境、世界各地供这些脚本运行的强大服务器，以及许多其他美味佳肴。 但是也有一些陷阱，其中之一就是Google 的配额和限制。 如果超出配额或限制，您的脚本将抛出异常并停止执行。” 例如，URL Fetch 调用限制为每天 20,000 次，同时执行脚本的限制为 30 个用户。 这意味着如果机器人的用户数量变得足够大，它将停止工作。 怎么办？

      例如，您可以购买自己的服务器或租用服务器并处理其上的所有逻辑。 它需要额外的金钱和时间，因此考虑其他事情是合乎逻辑的。

      出路

      开发人员可能会提出以下建议。 由于 Web 应用程序代表我运行，因此我的配额已被消耗。 但是，如果您不是代表我而是代表用户部署它怎么办？ 感兴趣的用户只需单击 3 次即可完成此操作。 你只需要给他一个嵌入了脚本的工作表模板。 他将为自己制作一份工作表副本，将脚本部署为 Web 应用程序并将 webAppUrl 插入所需的单元格。 现在配额和限制将不再由开发者使用，而是由用户使用。 而且这个用户每天在他的 Telegram 组中的消息不太可能超过 20,000 条。 一切似乎都奏效了。

      它不在那里！

      又是陷阱

      每个开发人员都希望自己的创造力成果有用。 对他和用户都是如此。 如何分发我们的脚本以便感兴趣的用户知道它？ 你可以在主题网站上写文章，在谷歌上做广告等等。这一切都很棒，但不使用免费工具是一种罪过。

      说到 Google 的好东西，不能不提到Google Workspace Marketplace 。 在那里，谷歌开发者为谷歌产品发布插件——工作表、文档、邮件等。


      于是，实现我们的Telegram bot的思路出现如下：

      1. 我们制作了一个 Google 工作表模板。
        它绘制了一个带有必要标题的界面，一个 Web 应用程序脚本附加到表中。

      2. 我们将所有消息处理逻辑从 web 应用程序转移到插件并在 Google Workspace Marketplace 上发布。

      3. 用户安装插件，为自己制作模板副本，进行初始设置（在所需的单元格中输入机器人令牌、Web 应用程序 URL、他的关键字）并使用它来满足自己的需求。 是的，这里我们将部分工作转移给了用户，但这就是我写这篇文章的原因。 也许阅读的人会提出解决方法。

        而且，它又没有了，这里还有陷阱。

      再次

      问题是您不能以任何方式将数据直接传递到附加组件中。 也就是说，您通过 webhook 收到了一条消息，但您无法将此消息发送给处理程序，让我提醒您，处理程序在附加组件中。 您不能运行附加组件内的函数。 你甚至不能传递一些标签或信号，比如“嘿，插件！ 有消息！ 运行 fetch(url) 并阅读！”。

      绝不。 我不知道为什么，是出于安全原因，还是出于其他原因。 你可以在这里阅读：如何将数据从 webapp 传输到插件。

      可能会想到这个想法：“我们在一张纸上写信息。 放置一个 onEdit() 或 onChange() 触发器，一旦将消息写入工作表，触发器就会触发，读取数据并按应有的方式处理所有内容。” 这里有 2 个陷阱。

      其中之一是所有 I/O 类型的电子表格操作都相对较慢——只有几分之一秒。 通过 Telegram 聊天中的密集通信，对工作表和来自工作表的多次调用可能会导致严重延迟。

      与这种情况相反，如果数据绕过工作表传递，直接从 fetch() 传递到附加代码。 谷歌的服务器速度很快，这样的操作需要几毫秒。

      第二个问题是 onEdit() 或 onChange() 触发器不会在工作表上的数据以编程方式更改时触发。

      如果用户更改了工作表上的数据，则会触发这些触发器。 例如，从键盘输入或从菜单中选择。

      更多关于这里：事件对象。

      有出口！

      仍然找到了堆栈上帖子中问题的答案。 作者甚至为正确答案分配了赏金，但他们当时向他建议的最好的事情是使用谷歌云服务。 是的，在美味的包子中有一个。 它当然是强大的东西。 这只是在超过某些指标时支付的。 而且我还没有看到好的手册。

      根据 GAS 的说法，有很多这样的资源。 这是Google Apps Script和mdn web docs等等。


      在 Google Cloud 上，此类资源要少得多。

      因此，当 Alan Wells 六个月后出现在帖子中并撰写有关 Sheets API 和 USER_ENTERED 的文章时，一切都立即变得清晰起来。

      方案如下：

      1. 在 Web 应用程序服务中启用 Sheets API。 如果您查看 appsscript.json 文件中的代码，您可以验证一切是否正确。 如果有人忘记了，那么为此，在脚本设置中，选中“在编辑器中显示 appsscript.json 清单文件”框。 它应该包含以下代码：

        "dependencies": { "enabledAdvancedServices": [{ "userSymbol": "Sheets", "serviceId": "sheets", "version": "v4" }]

        稍后，当用户复制您的模板时，此服务将包含在其脚本副本中。

      2. 我们通常如何将数据写入工作表？
        我们使用setValue(value) 。 像这样：

        var ss = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet(); varsheet = ss.getSheets()[0]; varcell = sheet.getRange("B2");

        入火！

        现在我们使用Google Sheets API 。

        代码应如下所示：
        var SHEETID = 'XXXXXXX'; function doPost(e) { var data = e.postData.contents; var rowValues ​​= [ [data, ""], ]; var request = { 'valueInputOption': 'USER_ENTERED', 'insertDataOption': 'INSERT_ROWS', 'data': [ { "range": "TEMP!A2:B2", "majorDimension": "ROWS", "values": rowValues, }, ], }; var response = Sheets.Spreadsheets.Values.batchUpdate(request, SHEETID);

        这里有两个关键点：
        一切，现在触发器将起作用。 数据，虽然是间接的 - 通过工作表，被传输到插件，整个电路功能。

        结论

        你可以在这里阅读更多关于这一切的信息：电报助手：描述和手册。 还有一个指向附加组件和带有脚本的电子表格的链接。 您可以自由使用所有这些。
        我将不胜感激建设性的批评和建议！

        格温达尔丹尼尔 - 路易斯·维拉斯奎兹—— Jan 28 '21 21 年 1 月 28 日



    原文:

    The topic of Telegram bots is only growing. If you're developing Google Sheets add-ons, you're writing Google App Script (GAS) code. Therefore, probably, the brains for these bots are written in GAS. There was an idea to write a bot that would look for keywords in chat messages and do something with these messages. For example, send me a notification that someone has written something on a topic that interests me. Or, on the contrary, delete obscene language and spam, and ban the authors of such messages in the chat.

    As always, in the process of practical implementation of the plan, pitfalls emerge. I will talk about some of them and ways to bypass them below. But first, some theory.

    getUpdates VS setWebhook?

    It doesn't matter what language you write the brains for your bot in - GAS, PHP, Pyton or something else. In any case, you are using the Telegram API.
    The API currently supports two ways to handle bot updates: getUpdates and setWebhook.
    getUpdates is a pull mechanism, setWebhook is a push mechanism.
    For example, you run getUpdates and get all the messages written in the chat during that hour. In GAS, you can set up a time trigger with this interval. But this means that for our example with an anti-spam bot, spam will hang in the chat with impunity for an hour. This is not good, it is advisable to shoot spam immediately on approach.
    Therefore it is necessary to use setWebhook. As stated in Marvin's Wonderful Guide to All Things Webhook:
    setWebhook, compared to getUpdates,

    • saves your bot from having to frequently request updates.
    • avoids the need for any polling mechanism in your code.

    setWebhook() + fetch(url)

    Now a few words about the mechanism for receiving updates or, more simply, messages from a Telegram chat.

    1. We write the simplest script in the GAS editor:

    function doPost(e) { var contents = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents); var chat_id = contents.message.from.id; // code for extracting data and writing to the sheet

    }


    That's it, we have an update in the contents variable. With the help of further processing, everything that is needed can be extracted from it. For example, chat_id is the identifier of the message sender, text is the text of the message.
    Then you can write this data on a sheet, send it to someone, etc.

    1. Let's deploy the script as a web application.
      At the same time, we specify the parameters “Run as: On my behalf, Who has access: Everyone” and copy the link to the web application (webAppUrl).

    2. We form a link. To do this, you need to connect the bot token and the web application URL as follows:

      var token = "1234567890:ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"; var webAppUrl = "https://script.google.com/macros/s/XXXXXXXX/exec"; var telegramUrl = "https://api.telegram.org/bot" + token;

      var telegramUrl = "https://api.telegram.org/bot" + token;

    3. Fetch the url and set the webhook:
      function setWebhook() { var url = telegramUrl + "/setWebhook?url=" + webAppUrl; var response = UrlFetchApp.fetch(url);

      }

    You can go to this url in the browser without any script and see
    {"ok":true,"result":true,"description":"Webhook is already set"}

    This is a JSON object, parsing which, you can extract all 3 fields separately - ok, result and description. Then they can be used in conditional logic, shown to the user and all that.

    Pitfalls

    So we have a script that instantly writes all messages from the Telegram chat to a sheet. Then you can search for keywords in them and do something with these messages. But imagine that you, delighted, have posted a link to this bot of yours in the public domain and expect that satisfied users will thank you for a useful tool.

    It wasn't there!

    Everyone is comfortable with Google services. And they provide you with sheets, and a script development environment, and powerful servers around the world on which these scripts work, and many other delicious goodies. However there are pitfalls and one of them is Google's quotas and limits. “Apps Script Services has daily quotas and limits on some features. If you exceed the quota or limit, your script will throw an exception and execution will stop.” For example, URL Fetch calls are limited to 20,000 per day, and the limit for simultaneously script executions is 30 users. This means that if the number of users of the bot becomes large enough, it will stop working. And what to do?

    For example, you can buy your own server or rent a server and process all the logic on it. It costs extra money and time, so it's logical to think about something else.

    Way out

    The developer may come up with the following. Since the web application is running on behalf of me, my quotas are consumed. But what if you deploy it not on my behalf, but on behalf of the user? An interested user can do this in 3 clicks. You just need to give him a sheet template with a script embedded in it. He will make a copy of the sheet for himself, deploy the script as a web application and insert the webAppUrl into the desired cell. Now quotas and restrictions will be consumed not by the developer, but by the user. And it is very unlikely that this user will have more than 20,000 messages per day in his Telegram group. Everything seems to work.

    It wasn't there!

    Pitfalls again

    Every developer wants the fruits of his creativity to be useful. Both for him and for the users. How to distribute our script so that interested users know about it? You can write articles on thematic sites, advertise on Google, etc. It's all great, but it's a sin not to use free tools.

    Speaking of Google goodies, one cannot fail to mention the Google Workspace Marketplace. There, Google developers publish add-ons for Google products - for sheets, documents, mail, etc.


    Thus, the idea of ​​​​implementing our Telegram bot appears as follows:

    1. We make a Google sheet template.
      It draws an interface with the necessary headings, a web application script is attached to the table.

    2. We transfer all the message processing logic from the webapp to the add-on and publish it on the Google Workspace Marketplace.

    3. The user installs the add-on, makes a copy of the template for himself, makes the initial settings (enters the bot token, web application URL, his keywords into the required cells) and uses it to his delight. Yes, here we shifted part of the work to the user, but that's why I am writing this article. Perhaps someone who reads will suggest workarounds.

      Moreover, it was not there again, there are still pitfalls here.

    Again

    The problem is that you can NOT pass data directly into an add-on in ANY WAY. That is, you received a message via the webhook, but you cannot send this message to the handler, which, let me remind you, is in add-on. You cannot run a function that is inside an add-on. You can't even pass some tag or signal like “Hey add-on! There's a message! Run fetch(url) and read!”.

    Never. I do not know why, whether for security reasons, or for some other reason. You can read about it here: How to transfer data from webapp to addon.

    The thought may come to mind: “We write messages on a sheet. Put an onEdit() or onChange() trigger and as soon as the message is written to the sheet, the trigger will fire, read the data and process everything as it should.” There are 2 catches here.

    One of them is that all I/O type spreadsheet operations are relatively slow - it's a fraction of a second. With intensive communication in the Telegram chat, multiple calls to the sheet and from the sheet can cause significant delays.

    In contrast to the situation, if the data is passed bypassing the sheets, directly from fetch() to the add-on code. Google's servers are fast and such operations take milliseconds.

    The second catch is that onEdit() or onChange() triggers do NOT fire when the data on the sheet is changed programmatically.

    These triggers are fired if the data on the worksheet has been changed by the user. For example, entered from the keyboard or selected from the menu.

    More about this here: Event Objects.

    There is an exit!

    The answer to the question in the post on the Stack was still found. The author even assigned a bounty for the correct answer, but the best thing that they suggested to him then was to use the Google cloud service. Yes, among the delicious buns there is one. And it's certainly powerful stuff. That's just it is paid when certain indicators are exceeded. And I have not seen good manuals for it.

    According to GAS there are plenty of such resources. This is Google Apps Script, and mdn web docs and a bunch more.


    On Google Cloud there are much fewer such resources.

    Therefore, when Alan Wells came to the post six months later and wrote about the Sheets API and USER_ENTERED, everything immediately became clear.

    The scheme is as follows:

    1. Enable the Sheets API in web application services. You can verify that everything is correct if you look at the code in the appsscript.json file. If anyone forgot, then for this, in the script settings, check the box “Show the appsscript.json manifest file in the editor”. It should contain the following code:

      "dependencies": { "enabledAdvancedServices": [{ "userSymbol": "Sheets", "serviceId": "sheets", "version": "v4" }]

      }


      Later, when the user does a copy of your template, this service will be included in its copy of the script.

    2. How do we usually write data to a sheet?
      We use setValue(value). Like this:

      var ss = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet(); varsheet = ss.getSheets()[0]; varcell = sheet.getRange("B2");

      cell.setValue(100);

    Into the fire!

    Now we use Google Sheets API.

    The code should look something like this:
    var SHEETID = 'XXXXXXX'; function doPost(e) { var data = e.postData.contents; var rowValues ​​= [ [data, ""], ]; var request = { 'valueInputOption': 'USER_ENTERED', 'insertDataOption': 'INSERT_ROWS', 'data': [ { "range": "TEMP!A2:B2", "majorDimension": "ROWS", "values": rowValues, }, ], }; var response = Sheets.Spreadsheets.Values.batchUpdate(request, SHEETID);

    }

    There are two key points here:
    A. We write to the sheet using batchUpdate().
    B. The 'USER_ENTERED' as it were, tells the trigger that the data on the sheet has been changed not by the program, but by the user.
    Everything, now the trigger will work. The data, albeit indirectly - through the sheet, is transferred to the add-in, and the whole circuit functions.

    Conclusion

    You can read more about all this here: Telegram Assistant: Description and manual. There is also a link to the add-on, and to the spreadsheet with the script. You can use all of this freely.
    And I will be grateful for constructive criticism and advice!

    Gwendal Daniel - Feb 12 '21

    Ravgeet Dhillon - Feb 22 '21

    Luis Velásquez - Jan 28 '21
item.author: bbaublys

item.title: #06：预览静态 Web 应用程序 | #06: Previewing Static Web Apps
item.description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 1, Day 6天！

我们快到第一周结束了！！ 到目前为止，我们已经了解了如何使用静态资产托管（Azure 静态 Web 应用程序）和无服务器 API （Azure Functions）功能以可扩展且经济高效的方式构建和部署 Web 应用程序。 我们学会了配置和保护我们的静态 Web 应用程序以满足我们的需求。 那么我们还需要谈什么？

部署选项？ 我们开始做吧！！ 到目前为止，我们已经讨论了默认体验：从生产分支部署，使用GitHub Actions for CI/CD。 但是我们如何在生产部署之前预览静态 Web 应用程序呢？ 让我们谈谈在拉取请求、非生产分支和预配置的命名环境中预览 SWA，这使我们的暂存工作流更有效率。

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 预览：在拉取请求中
  • 预览：在非生产分支中
  • 预览：在命名环境中
  • 部署：到自定义域
  • 练习：使用 SWA 预览功能查看 PR

关于作者

在 GitHub 上的 mishapos 中找到他

部署类型

当我们考虑部署时，我们通常会考虑两种环境：作为实际使用的主要端点的生产环境，以及可用于内部测试、预览版本的早期验证的暂存环境。

生产：这是由搜索引擎索引的真实世界部署端点，并与自定义域相关联（如果已配置）。

  • 拉取请求（PR） ：这是一个拉取请求的临时环境设置，并在 PR 关闭时拆除。
  • 分支：这是您可以为非生产分支设置的环境，在分支的生命周期内具有稳定的 URL。
  • 命名：上述环境具有反映其 PR（编号）或分支（名称）的 URL。 您还可以配置具有固定名称的稳定预览环境，与某些部署上下文相关联（例如，临时版本）

    • 生产部署具有像<DEFAULT-HOSTNAME>-<NUMBER>.azurestaticapps.net这样的 URL，其中DEFAULT-HOSTNAME对于每个应用程序都是唯一的。

    对于预览环境，URL 类似于： <DEFAULT_HOSTNAME>-<BRANCH_OR_ENVIRONMENT_NAME>.<LOCATION>.azurestaticapps.net其中LOCATION反映部署区域， BRANCH_OR_ENVIRONMENT_NAME也可以采用分支名称、命名环境或拉取请求中的数字。

    让我们简要地看一下其中的每一个。

    拉取请求

    目前，Pull Request预览环境可用于托管在 GitHub 中的项目，并通过 Azure 静态 Web 应用配置为使用 GitHub Actions：

    • 受监视分支的每个 PR 都会获得一个专用但临时的预生产暂存环境，当 PR 关闭时，该环境将被拆除。
    • 使用它来验证更改、执行完整性检查等。
    • 如果对与活动 PR 关联的分支进行了新提交，则会自动重建和部署环境。
    • 如果您的 GitHub 存储库是私有的，则分阶段环境是公开可见的事件 - 尽管默认情况下 URL 不容易被发现（即，不被搜索引擎索引）。

    了解如何在 Azure 静态 Web 应用中预览拉取请求。

    分支机构

    分支的预览环境将具有稳定的 URL。 例如，在 GitHub Actions 上下文中，这涉及两个步骤：

    • 将production_branch属性设置为要用作该生产部署源的分支。
    • 列出您希望在trigger (Azure Pipelines) 下预览环境或push: branches （在 GitHub 操作中）。

    如果要跟踪所有分支以获得预览环境支持，可以使用通配符（ **表示 GitHub Actions， *表示 Azure Pipelines）。

    这是一个示例 GitHub Actions 配置文件 -生产环境是从主分支构建的，并且为其他列出的分支（ dev和staging ）构建单独的预览环境。

    了解如何在 Azure 静态 Web 应用中创建分支预览环境。

     name: Azure Static Web Apps CI/CD on: push: branches: - main - dev - staging pull_request: types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, closed] branches: - main jobs: build_and_deploy_job: ... name: Build and Deploy Job steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v2 with: submodules: true - name: Build And Deploy id: builddeploy uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1 with: ... production_branch: "main"

    命名环境

    有时，您希望拥有一个非生产预览环境，该环境位于稳定的 URL（不绑定到特定的 PR 编号或分支名称），并且在提交到配置文件中所有跟踪的分支时重新构建。

    就像使用分支一样，这需要手动更改默认配置文件（GitHub Actions 或 Azure Pipelines）， 如本示例所示。

    在 GitHub Actions 案例中，步骤如下：

    • 将deployment_environment属性（在相关的构建作业中）设置为您要用于此预览环境的名称
    • 在push: branches - 提交到这些分支将导致重建/部署到此环境。

    这是一个示例 GitHub Actions 配置文件 - 它设置了一个名为release的命名环境，当对任何分支（由**通配符反映）进行更改时，该环境会更新，并部署到具有类似<DEFAULT_HOST_NAME>-release.<LOCATION>.azurestaticapps.net 。

    了解Azure 静态 Web 应用中的命名预览环境

    name: Azure Static Web Apps CI/CD on: push: branches: - "**" pull_request: types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, closed] branches: - main jobs: build_and_deploy_job: ... name: Build and Deploy Job steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v2 with: submodules: true - name: Build And Deploy id: builddeploy uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1 with: ... deployment_environment: "release"

    自定义域

    您可能已经注意到，默认部署 URL（用于生产环境的形式为XXX.azurestaticapps.net或用于预览环境的XXX.<LOCATION>.azurestaticapps.net ）对于使用和调用来说并不完全是用户友好的。

    添加自定义域会有所帮助。 在这里，给定一个类似www.azure.com的域， azure.com是顶级域， www是相关的子域。

    那么如何配置这些呢？ 你有两个选择：

    • 使用外部 DNS 提供商（如果您的域名注册商支持）
    • 使用Azure DNS （管理您的 DNS 域，即使在其他地方购买）

    为了使这篇文章简短，我们不会详细介绍每个选项。 相反，请根据您要进入的方向查看这些链接：

    操作方法：观看！

    更喜欢视频演练来了解该过程？ 我们为您介绍了Azure 提示和技巧：静态 Web 应用程序系列。 观看此视频，了解如何为您的静态 Web 应用设置自定义域！

    练习：试试看！

    拉取请求对于拥有高效的开源或多贡献者项目至关重要，因此了解 Azure 静态应用在设置预生产环境以验证拉取请求中提议的更改时的工作原理非常重要，然后再将其合并以进行生产部署.

    通过在现有 Azure 静态 Web 应用项目上完成本教程，获得该过程的实践经验。

    目前，Pull Requests 的分阶段预生产环境仅适用于 GitHub Actions 部署 - 因此请确保选择已设置默认工作流的 GitHub 托管的 SWA 项目。

    有用的资源



    原文:

    Welcome to Week 1, Day 6 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

    We're almost at the end of week 1!! 🎉

    So far, we've learned how we can use the static assets hosting (Azure Static Web Apps) and serverless API (Azure Functions) capabilities to build and deploy a web application in a scalable and cost-effective way. And we learned to configure and secure our Static Web App to suit our needs. So what else do we need to talk about?

    Deployment options? Let's do it!! We've talked about the default experience so far: deploy from a production branch, using GitHub Actions for CI/CD. But how do we handle previewing the Static Web App before production deploys? Let's talk about previewing SWA in pull requests, on non-production branches, and in pre-configured named environments that make our staging workflows more productive.

    What We'll Cover

    • What types of deployment does SWA support?
    • Previewing: in Pull Requests
    • Previewing: in Non-Production Branches
    • Previewing: in Named Environments
    • Deploying: to Custom Domains
    • Exercise: Review PR using SWA Preview Capability

    About the Author(s)

    Michail (Misha) Shaposhnikov is a Software Engineer at Microsoft working with the Azure Static Web Apps product amongst other things. Find him at mishapos on GitHub

    Deployment Types

    When we think about deployment, we are typically thinking of two kinds of environments: production which serve as the primary endpoint for real world use, and staging that can be used for internal testing, early validation of preview releases.

    Azure Static Web Apps has built-in support for four types of environments:

    • Production: This is the real world deployment endpoint that is indexed by search engines, and associated with the custom domain (if configured).
    • Pull Request (PR): This is a temporary environment setup for a Pull Request, and torn down when that PR is closed.
    • Branch: This is a environment you can set up for non-production branches, at a stable URL for the lifetime of the branch.
    • Named: The above environments have URLs that reflect their PR (number) or branch (name). You can also configure a stable preview environment with a fixed name, associated with some deployment context (e.g., an interim release)

    Production deploys have URLs like <DEFAULT-HOSTNAME>-<NUMBER>.azurestaticapps.net where the DEFAULT-HOSTNAME is unique for each application.

    With preview environments, the URL looks like: <DEFAULT_HOSTNAME>-<BRANCH_OR_ENVIRONMENT_NAME>.<LOCATION>.azurestaticapps.net where the LOCATION reflects the deployment region, and the BRANCH_OR_ENVIRONMENT_NAME can also take the form of a branch name, a named environment, or a number in the case of pull requests.

    Let's take a brief look at each of these.

    Pull Requests

    Currently Pull Request preview environments are available for projects hosted in GitHub, and configured to use GitHub Actions by Azure Static Web Apps:

    • Every PR to a watched branch gets a dedicated but temporary pre-production staging environment that is torn down when the PR is closed.
    • Use it to validate changes, perform sanity checks, etc.
    • The environment is automatically rebuilt and deployed if new commits are made to the branch associated with an active PR.
    • The staged environment is publicly visible event if your GitHub repo is private - though the URL is not easily discoverable (i.e., not indexed by search engines) by default.

    Learn how to preview Pull Requests in Azure Static Web Apps.

    Branches

    Preview environments for branches will have stable URLs. Configure them in the relevant GitHub Actions or Azure Pipelines workflow files as shown in this example.

    For example, in a GitHub Actions context, this involves two steps:

    • set the production_branch property to the branch you want to use as the source for that production deployment.
    • list all other branches that you want preview environments for under trigger (Azure Pipelines) or push: branches (in GitHub Actions).

    You can use a wildcare (** for GitHub Actions, * for Azure Pipelines) if you want to track all branches for preview environment support.

    Here's an example GitHub Actions config file - the production environment is built from the main branch, and separate preview environments are built for the other listed branches (dev and staging).

    Learn how to create branch preview environments in Azure Static Web Apps.

    name: Azure Static Web Apps CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - dev
      - staging
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, closed]
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build_and_deploy_job:
    ...
    name: Build and Deploy Job
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          submodules: true
      - name: Build And Deploy
        id: builddeploy
        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
        with:
          ...
          production_branch: "main"

    Named Environments

    Sometimes, you want to have a non-production preview environment that is at stable URL (not tied to a specific PR number or branch name), and that gets rebuilt on commits to all tracked branches in the configuration file.

    Just like with branches, this requires a manual change to your default configuration file (GitHub Actions or Azure Pipelines) as shown in this example.

    In the GitHub Actions case, the steps are:

    • Set the deployment_environment property (in the relevant build job) to be the name you want to use for this preview environment
    • List the branches you want associated with this named environment under push: branches - commits to those will result in rebuild/deploy to this environment.

    Here's an example GitHub Actions configuration file - this sets up a named environment called release which gets updated when changes are made to any branch (reflected by the ** wildcard), and deployed to a site with a URL like <DEFAULT_HOST_NAME>-release.<LOCATION>.azurestaticapps.net.

    Learn about named preview environments in Azure Static Web Apps

    name: Azure Static Web Apps CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - "**"
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, closed]
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build_and_deploy_job:
    ...
    name: Build and Deploy Job
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          submodules: true
      - name: Build And Deploy
        id: builddeploy
        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
        with:
          ...
          deployment_environment: "release"

    Custom Domains

    You may have noticed that default deployment URLs - in the form XXX.azurestaticapps.net for production environments or XXX.<LOCATION>.azurestaticapps.net for preview environments - are not exactly user friendly for use and recall.

    Adding a custom domain helps. Azure Static Apps makes that easy with options to also configure subdomains and an apex domain! Here, given a domain like www.azure.com, azure.com is the apex domain and www is the relevant subdomain.

    So how do you configure these? You have two options:

    • Use an external DNS Provider (if your domain registrar supports it)
    • Use Azure DNS (to manage your DNS domain, even if purchased elsewhere)

    To keep this post short, we won't go into details for each of these options. Instead check out these links based on the direction you want to go in:

    How-Tos: Watch It!

    Prefer a Video Walkthrough to understand the process? We have you covered with the Azure Tips And Tricks: Static Web Apps series. Check out this video to understand how you can setup a custom domain for your Static Web App!

    Exercise: Try it!

    Pull Requests are critical to having a productive open source or multi-contributor project, so it's important to know how Azure Static Apps works in setting up pre-production environments for validating the changes proposed in a pull-request, before merging it for production deployment.

    Get hands-on experience with the process by completing this tutorial on an existing Azure Static Web Apps project.

    At present, the staged pre-production environment for Pull Requests is available only for GitHub Actions deployments - so make sure you pick a GitHub-hosted SWA project that already had the default workflows set up.

    Useful Resources
item.author: nitya

item.title: 第 29 天：富有成效的星期六 | Day 29: Productive Saturday
item.description:
伊瓦德·伊夫·哈比马纳

发表于你好！
这个星期六是一个特别的星期六，我通常把我所有的星期六都浪费在没有得到高质量休息的地方，但不是这个。 今天，我能够完成很多比我平时能够完成的事情。 即使昨天我几乎彻夜未眠，我醒来时精力充沛，这在周末是很少见的，我能够改进我的论文并与我的朋友和队友一起完成我的个人项目。 我能够使用Jest和React-testing-library建立反应测试环境，并且在这个过程中我学到了很多东西，我肯定需要写一篇文章。 说到写作，我认为我认为这将更具挑战性，因为我似乎可以更快地完成任务，因此他们可能会写很多文章。 但我会尽量腾出更多时间来写作
明天我打算完成论文，学习或完成关于github actions的任务如果我有空我将尝试创建一篇关于Jest和RTL的文章
另外，明天将是连续上月的第一个月，我想我有很多东西要分享和记录，我从这次旅程中学到了很多东西，但现在我认为今天是一个很好的

我要注销了……

凯蒂休斯（她/她） - Apr 16 4月16日



原文:

Ivad Yves HABIMANA

Posted on May 8

Hi!
This Saturday was a special one and I usually waste all my Saturdays where I don't get quality rest but not this one. today I was able to accomplish many than I usually able to. even if yesterday I stayed up almost all night I woke up energetic which is rare for the weekends and I was able to improve on my paper and worked on my personal project with my friend and teammate. I was able to set up react testing environment with Jest and React-testing-library and I learned a lot in the process which I definitely need to write an article about. speaking of writing I think I think it's going to be more challenging because I seem to be faster working through task so that they may be many to write articles about. but I'm going to try to spare more time for writing
tomorrow I plan to finish on the paper and learn or finish the task about github actions if I spare time I will try to create an article about Jest and RTL
Also, tomorrow will be mu fist month of heacy streak and I think I have many to share and document that I learned from this journey but for now I think today was a good one

I'm signing offf....

Katie Hughes (she/her) - Apr 16
item.author: yvad60

item.title: Birdle：使用 Appwrite 构建的中型克隆 | Birdle: A Medium Clone built with Appwrite
item.description:
它是在 Appwrite 和 Nuxt3 的强大支持下构建的。 我曾尝试使用 NoSQL 数据库通过 Appwrite 实现媒体的一些功能。

在这里查看演示： Birdle 演示
注意：请使用电脑或笔记本电脑，因为该应用程序并未针对移动设备进行全面优化。

构建这个项目背后的想法是同时使用 Nuxt3 和 Appwrite 并学习它们。

由于 Nuxt 仍处于测试阶段，我不想失去这个为未来学习它的机会。

听说过 Appwrite 但没用过，最近几天把它部署到云端，建了个应用，部署了。 学到了很多关于 Appwrite 的知识，社区很棒。

特征

  • 用户可以使用magic-link和Google OAuth进行注册。
  • 关注用户并从他们那里获取最新文章。
  • 创建故事并发布它们。
  • 上传用户头像
  • 创建博客文章、发布和制作草稿
  • 博客文章上的 CRUD
  • 创建出版物
  • 在仪表板上获取最新的博客文章
  • 查看以下所有用户
  • 为每篇文章添加 5 个标签
  • 可以使用 /tag/[tag_name] 路由搜索每个标签
  • 编辑用户设置
  • 管理出版物
  • 检查已发布和草稿的故事。
  • 创建和编辑出版物

技术栈


 | Tech | Purpose | | ----------- | ----------- | | Nuxt3 | Frontend | | Tailwind CSS | Text | | SendInBlue | Send Emails | | Vercel | Deployment |

我没想到Appwite在第一次遇到时会那么容易。 让 Appwrite 很棒的一件事是它的文档和易于使用的 API。

在这个应用程序中，我使用了 Magic-link 和 Google 身份验证，这是一个用于存储帖子、用户数据、出版物以及与博客帖子和用户相关的所有媒体的存储的数据库。

要获取带有姓名首字母的用户的图像和姓名，Avatar API 是福音。 有许多第三方 API 可提供此服务，但集成它对 Appwrite 来说是一个加分项。

我在我的项目中主要使用 Vue3。 在构建Birdle时，我学到了很多关于 Nuxt3 的东西。 我遇到了很多问题，但最后通过社区或深入研究 Nuxt 代码库找到了解决方案。

与 Medium 一样，Birdle 也提供添加文章的支持。 我想构建一个类似于 Medium 的编辑器，但没有足够的时间来解决所有的麻烦，所以我使用了 TinyMCE。 它达到了目的，在未来，我将为 Birdle 创建 Medium 编辑器。

我只使用 Tailwind 构建了整个 Birdle 网络应用程序，我很高兴它变得很棒。

我希望在用户注册后立即发送电子邮件。 但 Appwrite 中不再存在默认邮件程序。 所以，我使用 SendinBlue 发送电子邮件。

未来的改进

  • 个人帖子的统计信息
  • 移动界面实现
  • 添加发布成员功能
  • 通知
  • 最初的媒体编辑器
  • 实现全局标签收集
  • 列表的实现
  • 人物、出版物和文章的搜索功能

提交类别

链接到代码

这里使用的技术栈是前端的 Nuxt.js 和 Tailwind css，后端的 appwrite。

鸟儿截图：

鸟单张图片

我们建议查看文档。

设置

确保安装依赖项

发展

在http://localhost:3000上启动开发服务器

生产

构建用于生产的应用程序：

查看部署文档。

其他资源/信息

截图和宣传视频

仪表盘

用户资料

单篇文章

出版物

发表于刊物

使用 TinyMCE 构建的编辑器

使用的数据资源

结论与思考

使用 Appwrite 很有趣。 在过去的 3 周里，我学到了很多关于使用 Appwrite 从头开始​​构建全栈应用程序的知识。

在 NoSQL 中构建应用程序对我来说是全新的。 总的来说，感谢 Dev.to 和 Appwrite 举办了这场精彩的黑客马拉松。



原文:

Birdle( pronounced as 'baerdle' ) is a clone of Medium.com webapp. it is built with an amazing backed of Appwrite and Nuxt3. I have tried to implement some of the features of medium with Appwrite using a NoSQL database.

Check out the demo over here : Birdle Demo
Note: Please use a computer or laptop as the app is not fully optimized for mobile devices.

The idea behind building this project was to use Nuxt3 and Appwrite together and learn both of them.

As Nuxt is still in beta, I did not want to lose this chance of learning it for the future.

I had heard of Appwrite but never used it, but in the last few days, I have deployed it on the cloud, built an app, and deployed. Learned a lot about Appwrite and the community is awesome.

Features

  • Users can register using magic-link and Google OAuth .
  • Follow users and get recent articles from them.
  • Create stories and publish them.
  • Upload user avatar
  • Create blog posts, publish and make a draft
  • CRUD on blog posts
  • Create publications
  • Get the latest blog articles on the Dashboard
  • Check out all the following users
  • Add 5 tags to each article post
  • Each tag can be searched using /tag/[tag_name] route
  • Edit user settings
  • Manage publications
  • Check published and draft stories.
  • Create & Edit Publications

Tech Stack 


| Tech          | Purpose     |
| -----------   | ----------- |
| Nuxt3         | Frontend    |
| Tailwind CSS  | Text        |
| SendInBlue    | Send Emails |
| Vercel        | Deployment  |

Appwrite - The Backend Guy

Appwrite has won me over firebase. I did not think of Appwite to be so easy in the first encounter. The one thing that makes Appwrite awesome is its documentation and the easy-to-use API.

In this app, I have used Magic-link and Google authentication, a database to store posts, user data, publications, and storage for all the media related to blog posts and users.

To get images and names of the users with their initials, Avatar API was the boon. There are many third-party APIs available that provide this service, but having it integrated is a plus for Appwrite.

Nuxt3 - The Front-end Guy

I do mostly use Vue3 in my projects. I learnt a lot of things about Nuxt3 while building Birdle. I encountered many problems but lastly found solutions either through community or digging myself into the Nuxt codebase.

TinyMCE - The Builder/Contractor

Like Medium, Birdle also provides support for adding articles. I wanted to build an editor similar to Medium but did not have enough time to go through all the hassle so I used TinyMCE for the purpose. It served the purpose and in the future, I will create the Medium editor for Birdle.

TailwindCSS - The House-keeping

Tailwind is my go-to tool for anything in CSS. I have built the whole Birdle web app using Tailwind only and I am happy that it turned out awesome.

SendInBlue - The Email Guy

I was looking to send emails as soon as the user is registered. but the default mailer is no more there in Appwrite. So, I used SendinBlue to send emails.

Future Improvements

  • Stats for individual post
  • Mobile UI implementation
  • Add Publication member functionality
  • Notifications
  • The original Medium Editor
  • Implement global tags collection
  • Implementation of Lists
  • Search functionality for People, Publications & articles

Submission Category

Web2 Wizards

Link to Code

{ pretty embed }

Birdle is a website similar to Medium.com and is built with Appwrite backend deployed on the cloud. The tech stack used here is Nuxt.js and Tailwind css on the frontend and appwrite on the backend.

Birdle Screenshots:

Birdle Single Post Image

Birdle User Profile Image

Birdle Dashboard Image

Nuxt JS Docs

We recommend to look at the documentation.

Setup

Make sure to install the dependencies

Development

Start the development server on http://localhost:3000

Production

Build the application for production:

Checkout the deployment documentation.

Additional Resources / Info

Screenshots & Promo Video

Dashboard

User Profile

Single Post Article

Publication

Post Published in Publication

Editor Built with TinyMCE

Resources for data used

Conclusion & Thoughts

It was fun working the Appwrite. In the past 3 weeks, I have learned a lot, about building a full-stack app from scratch using Appwrite.

Appwrite is amazing but I would love support for relational databases. It was new for me to build the app in NoSQL. Overall, thanks to Dev.to and Appwrite for hosting this amazing hackathon.
item.author: smppatel99999

item.title: 您应该使用 BDD 进行 API 测试吗？ | Should you use BDD for API testing?
item.description:
其中一些验收标准可能是自动化的。

详细的 API 测试（例如测试 CRUD 功能、错误代码、JSON 格式等）对业务来说很少有太大的兴趣，因此使用 Gherkin 来表达它们通常没有什么意义——它只会增加一层额外的复杂性。 使用任何 JavaScript 测试库测试 REST API 非常简单。 因此，如果您的团队发现使用空手道之类的工具编写 REST API 测试更有效，那就太好了！ 但这不是 BDD，也不是镇上唯一的游戏。

唯一的例外是您可以使用 Cucumber 作为跨团队开发人员之间的通信工具，作为描述和记录 API 合同的更具表现力的方式。 但是您也可以使用合同测试工具来做到这一点，尽管它可能不那么可读。

在评论中让我知道你的想法！



原文:

BDD is a collaboration practice: you describe business rules, examples of these rules, and acceptance criteria that illustrate these rules. Some of these acceptance criteria can be potentially automated.

Detailed API testing (e.g. testing CRUD functionalities, error codes, JSON format etc.) is rarely of much interest to the business, so it generally makes little sense to use Gherkin to express them - it just adds an extra layer of complexity. Lower level tools such as RestAssured do the job just fine. And testing REST APIs with any JavaScript test library is pretty trivial. So if your team finds it more efficient to write REST API tests with a tool like Karate, that's great! But it ain't BDD, and it's not the only game in town.

The only exception is where you might use Cucumber as a communication tool between developers across teams, as a more expressive way to describe and document API contracts. But you can do this with contract testing tools as well, though it may not be as readable.

Let me know your thoughts in the comments!
item.author: sabariiish

item.title: 错误：朋友还是敌人？？ (Probably a Foe) （可能是敌人） | Errors: Friend or Foe?? (Probably a Foe)
item.description:
我们都有过这样的经历，那个似乎并没有消失的错误。 对于我们大多数人来说，这是在我们学习的早期阶段。

出于某种奇怪的原因，您从 YouTube 教程中复制的代码似乎并没有像在线那样执行，而且您确定自己编写了相同的东西。 好吧，我带来了好消息和一些坏消息。

首先，坏消息......无论您的经验水平如何，您都会继续体验这种情况。 现在好消息是，你并不孤单。

每个人都会犯错，犯错是人之常情。 错误只是来自解释器或编译器的消息，告诉您您做错了什么。

当我第一次开始编程时，我一直对通过创建机器人来交易外汇、股票和加密市场感兴趣（如果你也有兴趣，请查看我的文章）所以我在 Udemy 上学习了这门很棒的 MQL4课程.

所以我准备迈出第一步，开始 MQL4 课程，下载并安装 MQL5 IDE。 在观看并准确输入他输入的内容之后。 我点击了“编译”按钮。


哈！ 我在那里（可能眼里含着泪水）有大约 30 个错误。 我很困惑。 我做错什么了？ 事实证明 MQL4 已被弃用，Metaquotes 平台不再支持它，因此我尝试使用 MQL5 编译器执行 MQL4 代码。 太棒了。

我不知道该怎么办。 我在谷歌上搜索了一些错误只是为了更加困惑。 我只是简单地关闭了我的笔记本电脑并上床睡觉。 问了一圈，朋友说我应该慢慢来，所以我决定从python开始。

我开始尝试新事物，阅读书籍，观看大量教程。 偶然发现了更多错误，但我的好伙伴 Google 和 StackOverflow 总是支持我。

最终，我决定再次尝试 MQL5，我编写了一个脚本并弹出了错误，但我已经从 python 中获得了如何阅读错误消息的经验。 花了几个星期，但我终于能够在我的移动平均脚本上修复这些语法错误。 耶。

是时候运行编译的代码了。 你猜怎么了？ 没有任何输出...代码中隐藏了逻辑错误。 逻辑错误比语法错误可怕得多。 为什么？ 它们不会被编译器或解释器拾取。

所以我不得不阅读我刚刚写的（并从互联网上复制的）......结果阅读代码比编写代码要困难得多。 更多谷歌搜索...更多 StackOverflow-ing... 长话短说。 我得到了我的简单移动平均线脚本。

但我注意到我在编码方面比几个月前要好得多。 我可以很容易地看出我错过了一个分号或没有关闭括号。 它变得更容易了。 直到今天我仍然犯错误，即使在写这篇文章时，我也可能犯了错误。 错误类型

  1. 语法错误：语法错误是源代码中的错误，例如拼写和标点错误、不正确的标签等，这些错误会导致编译器生成错误消息。 尝试控制台登录 rust 或在 c++ 中缺少分号或在 python 中不正确缩进，这些只是众多语法错误中的一部分。

  2. 逻辑错误：逻辑错误是程序员的推理错误，但不是编程语言的错误。 一个总是评估为真的循环就是一个例子。

  3. 执行错误：当程序被要求做一些它不能做的事情时，就会发生执行错误，从而导致“崩溃”。 运行时错误的广泛使用示例是要求程序除以 0。

处理错误的提示

  1. 谷歌是你最好的朋友：新手开发者和专业开发者之间的区别在于专业开发者知道在谷歌上搜索什么。

  2. 编写可读的代码：为未来的你省去试图理解你过去写的东西的压力。 正确注释，使用有意义的变量名称意味着当您在该变量中存储分数时不要使用x作为标识符。

  3. 使用简单的 IDE：对 Vi 没有冒犯（我也使用它），但在开始时，请使用对初学者友好的 IDE 和 IntelliSense，如 Vs-code。 在您已经知道如何退出编辑器之前不要使用 vi。

  4. 阅读文档：文档可能看起来很无聊，但它们是了解函数功能或变量用途的最佳方式。 作为开发人员，您必须是读者。

错误很烦人，我们都同意这一点。 尝试修复一个错误有时会导致 3 个全新的错误。 但是，这些错误仍然使我们成为了不起的开发人员。

有些人可能会认为他们是朋友，有些人可能会认为他们是敌人，但我塞缪尔·西蒙心智健全，故意和自愿地宣布我认为他们是敌人。
感谢您阅读到最后。 随意在评论中说出你的想法和意见。 建设性的批评总是受到赞赏。 祝你有美好的一天。



原文:

We've all had that experience, that one bug that just didn't seem to go away. For most of us, it was in our early stages of learning.

For some weird reason, the code you copied from a YouTube tutorial doesn't seem to execute the way it did online and you're certain you wrote the same thing. Well, I bring good news and some bad news.

First, the bad news... drum-rolls you'll keep experiencing this regardless of your level of experience. Now the good news, is you're not alone.

Everyone makes mistakes, to err is human. Errors are simply messages from the interpreter or compiler telling you that you did something wrong.

When I first started programming, I've always had an interest in trading the forex, stocks, and crypto market by creating bots (Check out my article on that if you're interested too) so I took this awesome course on MQL4 on Udemy.

So there I was, about to take that first step, Started the MQL4 course, and downloaded and installed the MQL5 IDE. After watching and typing exactly what he typed. I hit the "compile" button.


Ha! There I was (probably with tears in my eyes) with about 30 errors. I was so confused. What did I do wrong? Turns out MQL4 was getting deprecated and the Metaquotes platform wasn't supporting it anymore so I was trying to execute MQL4 code with an MQL5 compiler. Just wonderful.

I had no idea what to do. I searched some of the errors on google only to get more confused. I simply shut down my laptop and went to bed. I asked around and friends said I should take it slowly so I decided to start with python.

I started trying out new things, read books, and watched a ton of tutorials. Stumbled upon a lot more errors but my good buddies Google and StackOverflow always had my back.

Eventually, I decided to try out MQL5 once again, I wrote a script and errors popped up but I already got experience from python on how to read error messages. It took a couple of weeks, but I was finally able to fix those syntax errors on my moving average script. Yay.

Time to run the compiled code. Guess what? There wasn't any output... There were logical errors hidden in the code. Logical errors are a lot scarier than syntax errors. Why? They don't get picked up by the compiler or interpreter.

So I had to read what I just wrote (and copied from the internet)... Turns out reading code is a lot harder than writing code. More googling... More StackOverflow-ing... Long story short. I got my simple moving average script to work.

But I noticed I was far better at coding than I was months back. I could easily tell that I missed a semicolon or didn't close a bracket. It got easier. I still make mistakes to this day, even while writing this article, I probably made mistakes too. (Last time I checked I was human).

Types of Errors

  1. Syntax Errors: Syntax errors are mistakes in the source code, such as spelling and punctuation errors, incorrect labels, and so on, which cause an error message to be generated by the compiler. Trying to console.log in rust or missing a semicolon in c++ or not indenting properly in python, are just a number of the numerous syntax errors out there.

  2. Logical Errors: A logical error is a mistake in reasoning by the programmer, but it is not a mistake in the programming language. A loop always evaluating to true is an example.

  3. Execution Errors: An execution error occurs when a program is asked to do something that it cannot, resulting in a 'crash'. The widely used example of a run time error is asking a program to divide by 0.

Tips for Handling Errors

  1. Google is your best friend: The difference between a noob-dev and a pro-dev is that the pro-developer knows what to search for on google.

  2. Write readable code: Save future you the stress of trying to understand what past-you wrote. Comment properly, use meaningful variable names meaning don't use x as an identifier when you're storing a score in that variable.

  3. Use a simple IDE: No offense to Vi (I use it too) but when starting, use beginner-friendly IDEs with IntelliSense like Vs-code. Don't use vi until you already know how to quit the editor.

  4. Read the docs: Documentations may seem boring to read but they're the best way to know what a function does or what a variable is for. As a developer, you have to be a reader.

Errors are annoying, we can all agree on that. Trying to fix one error sometimes leads to 3 brand new errors. But still, these errors make us into the awesome developers we are.

Some might consider them a friend, some might consider them a foe but I Samuel Simon being of sound mind, willfully and voluntarily make this declaration that I consider them a foe.
😄😄

Thanks for reading to the end. Feel free to say your thoughts and opinions in the comments. Constructive criticism is always appreciated. Have a great day.
item.author: salviencode

item.title: PyScript :- JS 杀手？ | PyScript :- The JS Killer?
item.description:
瑞特什舒克拉

发表于我们都为了不同的目的而跳向不同的编程语言。 我们大多数人更喜欢使用 Python 进行机器学习和数据分析，而使用 JavaScript 进行 Web 开发。 将 ML 和数据分析项目添加到网站一直是开发人员的痛点。 我们需要创建和管理各种 API 来完成任务。

但是，情况不再如此。 现在，您可以像 JavaScript 一样使用 PyScript 在浏览器中编写和运行 Python。

借助 py-script 标签，您可以直接在 HTML 文件中编写 Python 程序，就像在 script 标签下编写 JavaScript 代码一样。 可以使用 py-env 安装模块。

安装： -

运行 PyScript 绝对不需要安装。 只需在 HTML 文件中添加适当的 CSS 和脚本导入语句，就可以开始了。 下面给出要添加的导入语句

<head> <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css" /> <script defer src= "https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js" ></script> </head>

注意： - 您需要等待一段时间，因为创建合适的环境来运行 python 文件需要时间。

如何使用 Pyscript： -

后者是首选，因为像 VS code 这样的代码编辑器仍然无法识别 HTML 文件中的 python 代码。 这可能会导致复杂代码中出现大量缩进错误，并且您将无法使用自动完成和建议等功能。

例子： -

入门

<!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css" /> <script defer src= "https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js" ></script> </head> <body> <py-script> print("Hello, I am written in Python") </py-script> </body> </html>

在 PyScript 中创建线性回归

为此，我们需要安装库 scikit-learn、matplotlib、NumPy 和 pandas。 可以通过在标签下添加库的名称来完成。

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css" /> <script defer src= "https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js" ></script> <py-env> - pandas - scikit-learn - matplotlib - numpy </py-env> </head> <body> <div id= "plot" ></div> <py-script src= "./1.py" output= "plot" ></py-script> </body> </html>
 import pandas as pd import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression data = { 'Hours' : [ 2.5 , 5.1 , 3.2 , 8.5 , 3.5 , 1.5 , 9.2 , 5.5 , 8.3 , 2.7 , 7.7 , 5.9 , 4.5 , 3.3 , 1.1 , 8.9 , 2.5 , 1.9 , 6.1 , 7.4 , 2.7 , 4.8 , 3.8 , 6.9 , 7.8 ], 'Scores' : [ 21 , 47 , 27 , 75 , 30 , 20 , 88 , 60 , 81 , 25 , 85 , 62 , 41 , 42 , 17 , 95 , 30 , 24 , 67 , 69 , 30 , 54 , 35 , 76 , 86 ]} s = pd . DataFrame ( data ) X = np . array ( s [ 'Hours' ]). reshape ( - 1 , 1 ) Y = np . array ( s [ 'Scores' ]). reshape ( - 1 , 1 ) X_train , X_test , y_train , y_test = train_test_split ( X , Y , test_size = 0.2 , random_state = 0 ) regressor = LinearRegression () regressor . fit ( X_train , y_train ) y_pred = regressor . predict ( X_test ) fig , plot = plt . subplots () print ( y_pred ) plot . scatter ( X_test , y_test , color = 'b' ) plot . plot ( X_test , y_pred , color = 'k' ) fig


图表和预测显示在浏览器上

结论： -

由于 PyScript 相对较新。 相当多的问题与它相关。

因此，自动缩进、自动完成等功能根本不起作用。 您还可以使用 PyRepl 创建交互式体验，它是 PyScript 项目的一部分。PyScript 具有巨大的潜力。 看看 PyScript 未来的发展会很有趣。

有关更多信息，请访问https://anaconda.cloud/pyscript-python-in-the-browser

胭脂红斯卡皮塔 - Apr 12 4月12日



原文:

Ritesh Shukla

Posted on May 7

We all have been hopping towards different programming languages for different purposes. Most of us prefer Python for Machine Learning and Data Analytics while JavaScript for Web development. The ability of JavaScript to run in the browser gives it an upper hand in web development. Adding ML and Data Analytics projects to a website has always been a pain for developers. We need to create and manage various APIs to get the task done.

However, this is not the case anymore. Now you can write and run Python in the browser using PyScript just like JavaScript.

PyScript: -

PyScript is developed by Anaconda. With the Help of py-script tag, you can directly write python programs in an HTML file just like you write JavaScript code under the script tag. PyScript also supports various python modules including scikit -learn, matplotlib. Modules can be installed using py-env.

Installation: -

There is absolutely no installation needed to run PyScript. Just add proper CSS and script import statement to the HTML file and you are good to go. The import statement to be added is given below

<head> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css" /> <script defer src="https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js"></script>
</head>

Note: - You need to wait for some time as it takes time to create the proper environment to run python files.

How to use Pyscript: -

1)Directly in HTML file (Embedded code). 

<py-script> print("Hello, I am written in Python")
</py-script>

2)Use external file 

<py-script src="./1.py" output="plot"></py-script>

Latter is preferred because code editors like VS code still doesn’t identify python code in HTML files. This can cause lots of indentation errors in complex codes and also you won’t be able to use features like auto-complete and suggestions.

Examples: -

Getting started 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css" /> <script defer src="https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js"></script> </head> <body> <py-script> print("Hello, I am written in Python") </py-script> </body> </html>

Creating Linear Regression in PyScript

For this, we need to install libraries scikit-learn, matplotlib, NumPy, and pandas. It can be done by adding the library's name under the tag.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css" /> <script defer src="https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js"></script> <py-env> - pandas - scikit-learn - matplotlib - numpy </py-env> </head> <body> <div id="plot"></div> <py-script src="./1.py" output="plot"></py-script> </body>
</html>

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
data={'Hours': [2.5, 5.1, 3.2, 8.5, 3.5, 1.5, 9.2, 5.5, 8.3, 2.7, 7.7, 5.9, 4.5, 3.3, 1.1, 8.9, 2.5, 1.9, 6.1, 7.4, 2.7, 4.8, 3.8, 6.9, 7.8], 'Scores': [21, 47, 27, 75, 30, 20, 88, 60, 81, 25, 85, 62, 41, 42, 17, 95, 30, 24, 67, 69, 30, 54, 35,76, 86]}
s=pd.DataFrame(data)
X=np.array(s['Hours']).reshape(-1,1)
Y=np.array(s['Scores']).reshape(-1,1)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, Y, test_size=0.2, random_state=0)
regressor = LinearRegression()
regressor.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = regressor.predict(X_test)
fig, plot = plt.subplots()
print(y_pred)
plot.scatter(X_test, y_test, color ='b')
plot.plot(X_test, y_pred, color ='k')
fig


Graph and prediction are displayed on the browser

Conclusion: -

Since PyScript is relatively new. Quite a few numbers of problems are associated with it.

a)Code editors are still not adapted to identify python code inside HTML files. So, features like auto-indent, auto-complete simply won’t work. b)You can’t read APIs because the SSL module is still not available. c)You can’t read CSV files from local storage.

d)It is relatively slow.

You can also create interactive experience using PyRepl which is part of PyScript project.PyScript has great potential. It would be interesting to see how things go in the future for PyScript.

For more information visit https://anaconda.cloud/pyscript-python-in-the-browser

Carmine Scarpitta - Apr 12
item.author: riteshshukla04

item.title: JavaScript 中的 Kaprekar 常量 | Kaprekar's Constant in JavaScript
item.description:
因此，前几天我在滚动 TikTok 时，我看到了其中一个视频中出现的数字6174 。 原来这是一个特殊的数字，称为 Kaprekar 常数，以印度数学家DR Kaprekar命名。

从一个至少有两个不同数字的四位数字开始。 如果一个数字少于四位，我们可以用零填充它。 例如， 42变为0042 。

  1. 将数字从大到小排列，再从小到大排列，得到最大值和最小值。
  2. 从最大值减去最小值。
  3. 重复这些步骤。

经过少量迭代后，结果总是会收敛到6174 。

例如，

628
8620 - 268 = 8352
8532 - 2358 = 6174

不过，手工完成这项工作很乏味。 让我们编写一些代码来进一步探索 Kaprekar 算法！

从概念到代码

核心算法可以用 JavaScript 实现如下。

首先，我们将给定的数字转换为用前导零填充的字符串：

 > var num = 423 ; > num . toString (). padStart ( 4 , ' 0 ' ); ' 0423 '

然后我们将数字拆分成一个数组，这样我们就可以将它们排列成我们的最小和最大数字。 数组操作是就地执行的，因此为每个值创建一个新数组：

 > var min = num . split ( '' ). sort () [ ' 0 ' , ' 2 ' , ' 3 ' , ' 4 ' ] > var max = num . split ( '' ). sort (). reverse () [ ' 4 ' , ' 3 ' , ' 2 ' , ' 0 ' ]

最后，我们使用join将每个数组转换回字符串，并使用parseInt将它们转换回数字，这样我们就可以减去这些值：

 > parseInt ( min . join ( '' )) 234 > parseInt ( max . join ( '' )) 4320

最终代码包含一个validate函数来拒绝没有多位数字的数字，并使用随机的四位数字自动运行我们的算法：

validate = function (num) { for (var i = 0; i < 10000; i += 1111) { if (num == i) { return false; } } return true; } kaprekar = function (num) { if (!validate(num)) { console.log("Invalid number " + num); return; } console.log(num); while (num != 6174) { var str = num.toString().padStart(4, '0'); var max = parseInt(str.split('').sort().reverse().join('')); var min = parseInt(str.split('').sort().join('')); num = max - min; console.log(num); } } var n = Math.random() * 10000 | 0; // from https://stackoverflow.com/a/61696576 kaprekar(n);

但是等等，还有更多！

这是该程序的一个变体，它生成所有四位数达到6174所需的多次迭代的直方图。

counts = {}; validate = function(num) { for (var i = 0; i < 10000; i += 1111) { if (num == i) { return false; } } return true; } kaprekar = function(num) { var count = 0; if (!validate(num)) { return; } while(num != 6174) { str = num.toString().padStart(4, '0'); max = parseInt(str.split('').sort().reverse().join('')); min = parseInt(str.split('').sort().join('')); num = max - min; count++; } counts[count] = counts[count] || 0; counts[count]++; } for (var i = 1; i < 10000; i++) { kaprekar(i); } console.log(counts);

运行此代码，您可以看到我们在最多七次迭代后达到了6174 ：

 { ' 0 ' : 1 , ' 1 ' : 383 , ' 2 ' : 576 , ' 3 ' : 2400 , ' 4 ' : 1272 , ' 5 ' : 1518 , ' 6 ' : 1656 , ' 7 ' : 2184 }

结论

就是这样！ 希望你喜欢这个有趣的小花絮。

请随时在评论中留下您的想法☀️😄👍



原文:

So I was scrolling TikTok's the other day, as one does, when I saw the number 6174 featured in one of the videos. Turns out this is a special number called Kaprekar's constant, named after Indian mathematician D. R. Kaprekar.

Start with a four-digit number with at least two different digits. If a number has less than four digits we can pad it with zeros. For example, 42 becomes 0042.

  1. Arrange the digits from largest to smallest and again from smallest to largest to get maximum and minimum values.
  2. Subtract minimum from maximum.
  3. Repeat these steps.

The result will always converge to 6174 after a small number of iterations.

For example,

628
8620 - 268 = 8352
8532 - 2358 = 6174

It's tedious to do this work by hand, though. Let's write some code to explore Kaprekar's algorithm further!

From Concept to Code

The core algorithm may be implemented in JavaScript as follows.

First we convert a given number to a string padded with leading zeros:

> var num = 423;
> num.toString().padStart(4, '0');
'0423'

Then we split the digits into an array so we can arrange them into our minimum and maximum numbers. The array operations are performed in-place, so a new array is created for each value:

> var min = num.split('').sort()
[ '0', '2', '3', '4' ]
> var max = num.split('').sort().reverse()
[ '4', '3', '2', '0' ]

Finally we use join to convert each array back into a string and parseInt to convert those back to numbers so we can subtract the values:

> parseInt(min.join(''))
234
> parseInt(max.join(''))
4320

JavaScript Implementation

The final code incorporates a validate function to reject numbers without multiple digits and runs our algorithm automatically using a random four-digit number:

validate = function (num) { for (var i = 0; i < 10000; i += 1111) { if (num == i) { return false; } } return true; } kaprekar = function (num) { if (!validate(num)) { console.log("Invalid number " + num); return; } console.log(num); while (num != 6174) { var str = num.toString().padStart(4, '0'); var max = parseInt(str.split('').sort().reverse().join('')); var min = parseInt(str.split('').sort().join('')); num = max - min; console.log(num); } } var n = Math.random() * 10000 | 0; // from https://stackoverflow.com/a/61696576 kaprekar(n);

But wait, there's more!

Here is a variation of the program that generates a histogram of many iterations it takes to reach 6174 for all four-digit numbers.

counts = {}; validate = function(num) { for (var i = 0; i < 10000; i += 1111) { if (num == i) { return false; } } return true; } kaprekar = function(num) { var count = 0; if (!validate(num)) { return; } while(num != 6174) { str = num.toString().padStart(4, '0'); max = parseInt(str.split('').sort().reverse().join('')); min = parseInt(str.split('').sort().join('')); num = max - min; count++; } counts[count] = counts[count] || 0; counts[count]++; } for (var i = 1; i < 10000; i++) { kaprekar(i); } console.log(counts);

Running this code you can see we reach 6174 after at most seven iterations:

{ '0': 1, '1': 383, '2': 576, '3': 2400, '4': 1272, '5': 1518, '6': 1656, '7': 2184 }

Conclusion

And that's it! Hope you enjoyed this fun little tidbit.

Please feel free to leave your thoughts in the comments ☀️ 😄 👍
